column6: Chase Shaun Mitchell (born August 29, 2006), professionally known as Che (previously cheRomani+), is an American recording artist and record producer from Atlanta, Georgia. He started making music in 2020. cheRomani+ era is all the songs made before the release of his first EP "3".

column6: After his four song mixtape, 3, Che started to promote the album in 2022 leading into 2023. Che released the lead single blac chyna, on February 10, 2023. blac chyna featured a different song to the previous song for the album, Intro. The switch in style resulted from the first version getting leaked in February of 2023. The leak included songs like Sip Mansion with Rich Amiri and Foreign with Yhapojj. There is no official tracklist for cc v1, fans have put together their own based off the files that leaked on February 6th.

column6: Che’s new style would lead to a new rendition of the project, completely different from the first version. This version almost exclusively featured CXO and Swishrr production. CXO and Che ran a Instagram broadcast channel to preview snippets to songs like fangs and bool. In Spring 2023 Che and his creative director whitecollarwill collaborated to give the album an identity. Closed Captions merch was previewed for months prior to the album’s release and it was highly desired by Che’s fanbase. The merch can be easily recognized by any underground listener. On July 14, 2023 Che released another lead single blueberry bakwood along with a music video. This track serves as the intro to the album. The album would release the next Friday with the merch alongside it. Che and fellow artist OsamaSon both released their projects the same day, using each other’s respective fanbases for promotion. Che went on to see massive success from this project and would fuel that into a tour with OsamaSon.

column6: Crueger is Che’s second mixtape and the sequel to Before Crueger. It takes its name from the antagonizing Freddy Krueger from the classic horror film series A Nightmare on Elm Street. Che would first confirm the project’s existence and its title on August 26, 2023, in an interview with BoxBoys.

column6: Che spent months teasing the album, previewing many songs on social media and collaborating with influencers to draw more eyes to his rollout. On June 29, he would appear on stream with Young Dabo and preview “ENJOY YOUR LIFE” and “Children Shouldn’t Play With Dead Things,” both of which became fan favorites during the buildup to the album’s release. Che next participated in plaqueboymax’s popular Song Wars series on Twitch, where he would again preview “ENJOY YOUR LIFE.”

(Aug 30th, 2024) (Sayso Says is officially released on all platforms)
(Jan 17th, 2025) (Che capitalizes the "c" in Che)
(Feb 8th, 2025) (Che posts IG and Twitter posts with the caption "REST IN BASS")
(Jun 26th, 2025) (Che shows off the cover art for REST IN BASS and the album gets delayed due to the reception of the art)
(Jul 18th, 2025) (REST IN BASS is officially released on all platforms)
column6: On December 17th, 2024, antibain, Che’s VFX Editor and close affiliate, first mentioned the album. In Feburary 2025, Che would go on to announce the name of the project. He would do this through tweeting the name, changing his Instagram name, and creating a new Instagram account. A teaser to the album would be created by close affiliate PoppyGavin showcasing the album’s logo, and video clips of the music video to his song “Love (MKB),” which would release as a single on March 16th paired with a music video. On June 24, 2025, it was announced that REST IN BASS was rumored to see an official release on June 27th. Then on June 26, it was announced that REST IN BASS would not be dropping that Friday and would be delayed due to the controversy of the artwork. It would later release on July 17th, 2025 after being announced on July 14th by his close affiliate whitecollarwill.

column6: Che began teasing that there was going to be a deluxe to REST IN BASS. In October, Che dropped a trailer and multiple snippets via several Instagram posts, teasers and performing songs that would be on the deluxe on his Rest In Bass US Tour. The deluxe was announced by @Kurrco on Twitter on October 1st 2025. On December 13th, 2025, track 7 "DIRTY SPRITE" was previewed onto Instagram. On December 24th, 2025, Che would post on his Twitter announcing that the deluxe was dropping on Christmas. It ended up releasing the very next day on December 25th, 2025.

Tracker Guidlines

Remember to use the right formatting. If you feel like something is missing or you did something wrong, copy something a previous submission has already done and use that as the guideline or just ask someone.
No fan remakes, no unofficial mixtapes, nothing that's commercially available, always upload to a site that can be streamed off of.
Make sure you use alternating colors and check often that they are not broken (Format, Alternating Colors, choose white and the grey that has one grey in between it and white.)

To edit anything in Best Of, Special, Grails, Wanted, Worst Of, Recent, Recently Recorded, or OG Files, edit it in Unreleased. Those sections work as filters, filtering through the Unreleased section by adding all songs that meet the corresponding emoji or criteria, such as Leak Date, File Date, or the OG File having an Available Length.
No released remixes, no matter how obscure, should be on this page. Those should be in Misc / Released.

All music video editions, live freestyles, and released remixes go in Misc / Released.
Check if the song/mv/whatever is already added before you add it.

Use Pillowcase and imgur.gg for all links, because KrakenFiles and PixelDrain links are deleted after some time. If something can't be uploaded to Pillowcase or imgur due to large file size, then use Mega.

Try to get the highest quality file or snippet possible, and if it's a snippet try to get the longest snippet and not one that could be missing part of the song. Always link OG files if it has one leaked.
